description: Capabilities document declares limits.max_embedded_bytes != 65536. RFC-ACDP-0002 §6.3 fixes the embedded-bytes ceiling at 64 KiB (65536 bytes) for the entire v0.1.0 spec; the schema enforces this with `const: 65536` (acdp-capabilities.schema.json line 92). A registry advertising any other value is non-conformant — consumers cannot rely on the documented embedded-size invariant.

expected.consumer_action: MUST NOT proceed against this registry. A producer relying on the spec's 64 KiB invariant could otherwise build a publish request the registry would later reject (or worse, accept an oversized embedded blob a v0.1.0 consumer would reject).
expected.rationale: max_embedded_bytes is fixed by the spec, not negotiated per-registry. Schema-level enforcement (`const`) means standard JSON Schema validators catch this without requiring a custom rule.
